title: Daily Market Digest: June 22, 2026
subtitle: Market closes mixed amidst geopolitical developments and Fed rate hike concerns

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Exhibit Mixed Performance on Monday, Driven by Tech Gains and Geopolitical Optimism
items.summary: U.S. equities saw a mixed performance today, with major indices showing modest movements. Optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran peace agreement and continued strong demand for artificial intelligence (AI) in the semiconductor sector provided underlying support. However, investor sentiment was tempered by lingering concerns over future Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Oil prices experienced a notable decline amidst the diplomatic progress, while key economic data points, including jobless claims and GDP projections, offered a clearer picture of the economic landscape. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Apogee Therapeutics Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: Shares of Apogee Therapeutics ($APGE) jumped significantly after AbbVie announced plans to acquire the biotech company for $10.9 billion in cash.

items.headline: Definium Therapeutics Leads Biotech Surge
items.summary: Definium Therapeutics was reported as a top mover among equities with at least a $2 billion market cap, contributing to a strong day for the biotech sector.

items.headline: Super Micro Continues Strong Performance
items.summary: Super Micro Computer ($SMCI) continued to lead the S&P 500's positive movers, reflecting ongoing strength in technology.

items.headline: SpaceX Declines After Bond Offering
items.summary: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. ($SPCX) shares fell after the company announced a new bond offering.

items.headline: Alamos Gold Hit by Retreat in Precious Metals
items.summary: Alamos Gold experienced a significant decline, making it one of the worst performers among companies with a market cap of at least $2 billion, as gold spot prices retreated.

items.headline: Moderna Faces Steep Declines in Trading
items.summary: Moderna shares faced steep declines, making it one of the hardest hit in the S&P 500 today.

items.headline: Technology and Financials Lead Gains, Energy and Communication Services Lag
items.summary: Seven out of 11 major S&P 500 sectors were in the green, with Financials leading gains, up 1%. Technology also performed strongly, driven by semiconductor shares surging on AI demand. The Technology Select Sector SPDR ($XLK) advanced 2.7%. Conversely, Communication Services (.SPLRCL) was a laggard, falling 2.3%, and the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) declined 1.7% as oil prices fell amid U.S.-Iran diplomatic progress.

items.headline: Fed Signals Potential Future Rate Hikes as Inflation Persists
items.summary: Following last week's FOMC meeting, the Federal Reserve maintained interest rates at 3.50% to 3.75% but removed its easing bias, signaling a hawkish shift. With inflation running hotter than expected (May CPI up 4.2% year-over-year), most FOMC members now foresee at least one rate hike before the end of 2026. Markets are pricing in a high probability of a hike by September.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Decrease, Q1 GDP Holds Steady
items.summary: Initial jobless claims for the week ending June 13 decreased by 4,000 to 226,000. The final reading of Q1 2026 GDP is expected to hold at 1.6% SAAR, while the Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model tracks Q2 2026 at 3.0% SAAR, indicating resilient economic momentum. The Conference Board Leading Economic Index for the U.S. also rose slightly by 0.1% in May, supported by financial components.

items.headline: Micron Technology's Q3 Earnings in Focus This Week
items.summary: The earnings season continues with several key reports expected this week. All eyes will be on Micron Technology ($MU) as it reports its fiscal Q3 earnings on Wednesday, with revenue anticipated to surge due to high demand for AI memory. Nvidia ($NVDA) is also holding its annual shareholder meeting, keeping the semiconductor sector in the spotlight. FedEx ($FDX) and Darden Restaurants ($DRI) are also scheduled to report later in the week.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Diplomacy Eases Oil Prices and Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Progress in U.S.-Iran negotiations has led to a significant drop in crude oil prices, easing global inflation anxieties. This geopolitical development has positively influenced investor sentiment, despite ongoing concerns about domestic inflationary pressures and the Federal Reserve's stance.
